passed: POST /auth/register creates user and returns tokens
POST /auth/login with correct credentials returns tokens
GET /devices returns all registered devices with is_online
POST /devices/{uuid}/commands queues command for REST device
GET /device-gateway/{uuid}/commands/next second call returns null (queue empty)
POST /device-gateway/{uuid}/command-ack updates device state in Firebase
POST /device-gateway/connect with empty devices dict returns 200 (silent no-op)

failed.actual: Empty list — filter uses wrong key 'device_type' instead of 'type'
404 Device not found — route shadowed by /{device_uuid}

failed.test: Frontend displays device name and room
failed.expected: Device cards show name and room labels
failed.actual: Name and room are undefined — backend drops these fields
Blank room button, React key warning for undefined

failed.test: POST /device-gateway/connect with null device_uuid generates UUID server-side
failed.expected: Server generates UUID and returns in generated_uuids
failed.actual: 422 validation error — DeviceSchema requires str, rejects null
Any string accepted — 'HACKED' persisted to Firebase as last_command_status
500 error — DB is in europe-west1 region, not default US. Required URL: https://interactive-house-d3e5c-default-rtdb.europe-west1.firebasedatabase.app

critical.title: Firebase service account private key on disk
critical.description: Full RSA private key, private_key_id, client_email, and client_id for the interactive-house-d3e5c Firebase project are present on disk. Anyone with repo access has full Admin SDK access to the Firebase Realtime Database.
critical.recommendation: Rotate the service account key immediately via Firebase Console. Ensure .gitignore covers the file and verify it was never committed to git history.

critical.title: Weak hardcoded JWT secret key in .env
critical.description: SECRET_KEY is set to 'super-secret-jwt-key-change-me'. Any token signed with this key can be forged offline. HS256 with a weak secret is equivalent to no signature.
critical.recommendation: Replace with at least 256 bits of random entropy: python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"

critical.title: Live Firebase project credentials in .env
critical.description: Live FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID and FIREBASE_DATABASE_URL values are in the .env file. Combined with C-1, this provides full database access.
critical.recommendation: Never commit .env to version control. Use .env.example with placeholder values only.

high.title: optional_user always returns None — all device routes unprotected
high.description: The optional_user dependency unconditionally returns None without validating any token. Every device route uses Depends(optional_user), meaning all device listing, reading, deleting, and commanding is open to any unauthenticated caller. current_user exists and works but is never used.
high.recommendation: Replace optional_user with current_user on all device endpoints, or implement proper API key validation.

high.title: Unauthenticated DELETE endpoint for devices
high.description: DELETE /api/v1/devices/{device_uuid} uses optional_user (always None). Anyone can permanently delete any device from Firebase with no credentials.

high.title: Firebase path injection via unsanitized device_uuid
high.description: Paths are constructed as f"{DEVICES_PATH}/{device_uuid}" with no validation. A device_uuid containing '/' traverses outside the devices/ subtree and can read, write, or delete arbitrary Firebase nodes (e.g. '../../users/admin').
high.recommendation: Validate device_uuid format — reject any value containing '/', '.', '#', '$', '[', or ']' (Firebase forbidden characters). Use UUID format validation.

high.title: Device gateway routes have zero auth dependency
high.description: Unlike other routes that at least declare optional_user, device gateway routes have no auth dependency at all. Any caller can connect arbitrary devices, poll commands, and send acks.
high.recommendation: Implement device API key or pre-shared secret authentication for gateway endpoints.

high.title: API_KEY loaded from settings but never enforced
high.description: api_key: str is declared in Settings and loaded from .env, but is never referenced in any route, dependency, or middleware. Creates a false sense of security.
high.recommendation: Implement API key middleware or dependency that validates the API_KEY header on protected routes.

high.title: _build_device() drops name and room fields — frontend shows undefined
high.description: _build_device() only keeps device_uuid, type, transport, capabilities, state, status, and last_seen. Fields like name and room sent in the connect payload are silently discarded. The frontend's mapDevice() reads d.name and d.room which are always undefined.
high.recommendation: Add name and room to _build_device() return dict, or add them as fields to DeviceSchema.

high.title: list_devices() filter uses wrong key — type filter always returns empty
high.description: Filter uses d.get('device_type') but Firebase stores the field as 'type' (set in _build_device line 36). GET /devices?type=light always returns an empty list.
high.recommendation: Change d.get('device_type') to d.get('type') on line 61.

high.title: /events route shadowed by /{device_uuid} — endpoint unreachable
high.description: GET /{device_uuid} is registered on line 20 before GET /events on line 48. FastAPI matches routes in registration order, so /devices/events is captured by /{device_uuid} with device_uuid='events', returning 404.
high.recommendation: Register /events before /{device_uuid}, or move to a distinct prefix like /device-events.

medium.title: Wildcard CORS — allow_origins=['*']
medium.description: All origins accepted with allow_methods=['*'] and allow_headers=['*']. Any web page from any domain can make cross-origin requests to the API.
medium.recommendation: Restrict to known origins (localhost:3000 for dev, production domain for prod).

medium.title: DeviceSchema.device_uuid requires str but connect_device expects to handle null
medium.description: device_uuid is typed as str (required). device_service.connect_device() has logic to generate UUID when null, but Pydantic rejects null with 422 before the service layer runs. The null-UUID code path is unreachable.
medium.recommendation: Change to device_uuid: str | None = None in DeviceSchema.

medium.title: CommandAckPayload.status has no enum constraint — arbitrary strings accepted
medium.description: status: str = 'ok' accepts any string. Tested with 'HACKED' — it was accepted (200) and persisted to Firebase as device.status.last_command_status.
medium.recommendation: Use Literal['ok', 'error'] or an enum to constrain valid values.

medium.title: Self-reported device_uuid can overwrite any existing device
medium.description: Any caller of POST /device-gateway/connect can supply any device_uuid including one belonging to an existing device, and it will overwrite that device's data. No ownership verification.
medium.recommendation: Implement device ownership or authentication tokens per device.

medium.title: mark_stale_devices_offline runs O(N) Firebase scan on every heartbeat
medium.description: Called synchronously inside heartbeat(). Performs _devices_ref().get() which reads ALL devices from Firebase. With N devices heartbeating every 5s, this creates N full-table scans per 5s interval.
medium.recommendation: Move to a background scheduled task (asyncio.create_task with periodic interval) instead of per-request.

medium.title: No refresh token endpoint — tokens non-renewable
medium.description: RefreshTokenRequest schema is defined in auth_schema.py and create_refresh_token is implemented in security.py, but there is no POST /auth/refresh route. JWTs expire and users have no recovery path short of re-logging in.
medium.recommendation: Add POST /auth/refresh endpoint that validates refresh tokens and issues new access tokens.

medium.title: React key warning — undefined room values in rooms array
medium.description: devices.map(d => d.room) produces undefined for devices without room field. The Set contains undefined, creating buttons with key={undefined} which triggers React's duplicate key warning.
medium.recommendation: Filter out undefined/null values: devices.map(d => d.room).filter(Boolean)

medium.title: Hardcoded ngrok URL in RVC simulator and Android client
medium.description: Both hardcode the same ngrok URL (knolly-svetlana-beribboned.ngrok-free.dev). Free ngrok URLs expire and rotate. Anyone else running the project will fail silently.
medium.recommendation: Read URL from environment variable or config file.

low.title: No rate limiting on any endpoint
low.description: No slowapi, fastapi-limiter, or middleware-based throttling found. Auth endpoints are exposed to brute-force attacks. Heartbeat and command polling endpoints are not rate-limited.
low.recommendation: Add rate limiting middleware, especially on /auth/login and /auth/register.

low.title: Dead code — unit_routes.py, state_store.py, events stub
low.description: Multiple files and endpoints exist as stubs or dead code. unit_routes.py defines routes never registered. state_store.py is blank. State controller returns TODO for all methods.
low.recommendation: Remove dead code or implement the stubs. Mark TODO endpoints as not-implemented with proper HTTP 501 status.

low.title: Double Settings() instantiation
low.description: security.py imports Settings class and creates a new instance instead of importing the existing singleton. Doubles .env file parsing on startup.
low.recommendation: Change to: from services.src.config.settings import settings (import the instance, not the class).

low.title: print() logging user IDs to stdout
low.description: print() calls log user_id and username on every user operation. In production, stdout may be captured by logging aggregators without access controls.
low.recommendation: Replace print() with the project's logger (get_logger) at DEBUG level.

environment_issues.title: Firebase database URL points to wrong region
environment_issues.description: The .env.example does not specify the correct Firebase Realtime Database URL. The database is hosted in europe-west1, requiring URL: https://interactive-house-d3e5c-default-rtdb.europe-west1.firebasedatabase.app. Using the default US URL causes 500 errors on all Firebase operations.
environment_issues.recommendation: Update .env.example with the correct regional URL or add a comment noting the region requirement.
